sections.paragraph:
Ζωτίων, ωνος, ὁ (usu. [ins, pap] spelled Σωτίων elsewh.) Zotion, an eccl. assistant in Magnesia IMgMai 2.

usu. usu. = usually

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

eccl. eccl. = ecclesiastical

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 432
